citations.description: Unit test only — no ASGI/TestClient harness for the wrong-resource-via-stub-verifier scenario
Integration tests present cover 200/401/403 but not the stub-verifier resource-mismatch case

findings.criterion: Integration test asserts: valid static token → 200; unknown/invalid token → 401; a principal lacking a required_scope → 403; and a token whose resource ≠ auth_resource_server_url (injected via a stub verifier in the composite) → 401 at the composite regardless of the sub-verifier (negative controls included).
findings.detail: The first three cases (200/401/403) are covered by integration tests (test_http_auth_valid_token_200_and_missing_token_401, test_missing_required_scope_403_and_present_200). However the fourth sub-criterion — 'a token whose resource ≠ auth_resource_server_url injected via a stub verifier in the composite → 401' — is tested only as a unit test (test_composite_rejects_wrong_resource_token at line 226–232), not as an integration test over the ASGI transport using a TestClient. The criterion explicitly requires an 'Integration test' for this case with 'negative controls included.'

citations.description: never is a non-raising acceptor; a raising-verifier-after-accepting control is absent

findings.criterion: Unit test asserts the composite contract: None → try next; an AccessToken with matching resource → accept; a verifier that raises → logged, non-acceptance, request denied (no swallow-to-accept), with a control where a raising verifier ordered after an accepting one is never consulted.
findings.detail: test_composite_contract_none_accept_raise_and_shortcircuit covers None→try-next, accept, raise→non-acceptance, and short-circuit via a non-raising acceptor (never.called is False). However the specific sub-criterion 'a control where a RAISING verifier ordered after an accepting one is never consulted' is not explicitly tested — the 'never' verifier in the test is a normal acceptor (_StubVerifier(result=_access_token(client_id='loser'))), not a stub configured to raise. No test in the file places a raising stub after an accepting one and asserts it is not called.

citations.description: Cannot run make lint / make typecheck / make test from the read-only repository tools; no CI result is recorded in the ticket comments.

findings.criterion: make lint && make typecheck && make test pass.
findings.detail: This criterion requires running the build toolchain; the outcome cannot be verified from repository content alone. The code is present and plausibly integrated, but test execution and static-analysis results are not observable via read-only file tools.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
